name: no_tool_calls
description: LLM returns content immediately with no tool calls â the agent loop completes in a single iteration.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is 2 + 2?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: 2 + 2 equals 4.

expected.result: 2 + 2 equals 4.

name: single_tool_call
description: Turn 1: LLM requests get_weather for Paris. Turn 2: after receiving the tool result, LLM returns a final content response.

messages.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

message.content: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

expected.result: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

message_sequence.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?
72Â°F sunny
The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

name: multiple_tool_calls_single_turn
description: Turn 1: LLM returns two parallel tool calls â get_weather for Paris and London. Both results appended. Turn 2: LLM summarises both.

messages.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
Compare the weather in Paris and London.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

message.content: Paris is 72Â°F and sunny, while London is 59Â°F and cloudy.

expected.result: Paris is 72Â°F and sunny, while London is 59Â°F and cloudy.

message_sequence.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
Compare the weather in Paris and London.
72Â°F sunny
59Â°F cloudy
Paris is 72Â°F and sunny, while London is 59Â°F and cloudy.

name: multi_turn_tool_calls
description: Three-turn chain: Turn 1 calls get_weather, Turn 2 calls convert_temperature with the result, Turn 3 returns the final answer.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant with access to weather and conversion tools.
What is the weather in Paris in Celsius?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city (returns Fahrenheit)

tools.name: convert_temperature
tools.description: Convert a temperature between Fahrenheit and Celsius

message.content: The weather in Paris is 22.2Â°C and sunny.

expected.result: The weather in Paris is 22.2Â°C and sunny.

name: max_iterations_exceeded
description: LLM returns tool calls on every turn for 11 iterations, exceeding MAX_ITERATIONS=10. The agent loop must raise an error.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Keep checking the weather forever.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

name: bindings_injected
description: Tool has a binding {unit: {input: 'preferred_unit'}}. The LLM omits the bound parameter. At execution time, the binding injects the value from the parent inputs, so the tool receives the merged arguments.

messages.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: The weather in Paris is 22Â°C and sunny.

expected.result: The weather in Paris is 22Â°C and sunny.
expected.notes: The binding {unit: {input: 'preferred_unit'}} resolves 'preferred_unit' from parent_inputs to 'celsius' and injects it into the tool call arguments before execution, overriding any LLM-provided value.

name: tool_not_registered_error
description: LLM calls a tool named 'unknown_tool' that has no registered handler in tool_functions. The agent loop must raise a ValueError.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Do something with an unknown tool.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

name: tool_result_message_format
description: After executing a tool, the tool result message appended to the conversation must have role='tool', content as a TextPart with the stringified result, and metadata.tool_call_id matching the original call id.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is the weather?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: It is 72Â°F and sunny.

expected.result: It is 72Â°F and sunny.

name: assistant_tool_calls_metadata
description: When the LLM returns tool calls, the assistant message appended to the conversation must have role='assistant', empty content, and metadata.tool_calls containing the full tool_calls array with id/type/function for each call.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Compare weather in two cities.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: Paris is warmer and sunnier than London today.

expected.result: Paris is warmer and sunnier than London today.

name: empty_tool_result
description: Tool function returns an empty string. The tool result message must still be sent to the LLM with empty content â it must not be skipped or filtered out.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Clear the cache.

tools.name: clear_cache
tools.description: Clear the application cache, returns empty on success

message.content: The cache has been cleared successfully.

expected.result: The cache has been cleared successfully.
expected.notes: Empty string result must NOT be skipped. The tool result message is still appended to the conversation so the LLM can see the tool was executed successfully.

name: async_tool_function
description: Agent loop correctly handles async tool functions

messages.content: You are helpful.
Look up the data

tools.name: lookup
tools.description: Look up data

message.content: I found: test data

expected.result: I found: test data

name: events_basic_tool_loop
description: Â§13.1 Events â A 2-turn tool-call loop emits the full event lifecycle: status â tool_call_start â tool_result â messages_updated â done.

messages.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

message.content: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

expected.result: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

data.response: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

expected.notes: Events must be emitted in this exact order. tool_call_start fires before tool execution; tool_result fires after. messages_updated fires when tool result messages are appended. done is always the last event.

name: events_no_tools
description: Â§13.1 Events â Single-turn completion with no tool calls. Only the done event is emitted.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is 2 + 2?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: 2 + 2 equals 4.

expected.result: 2 + 2 equals 4.
expected.notes: When the LLM returns content without tool calls, no tool_call_start/tool_result/messages_updated events fire. Only the done event is emitted.

name: events_error_logged
description: §13.1 Events  A tool function raises an exception during execution. The exception is caught and returned as an error string to the LLM. The loop continues normally.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_results.result: Error calling 'get_weather': RuntimeError: Weather service unavailable

message.content: Sorry, the weather service is currently unavailable.

expected.result: Sorry, the weather service is currently unavailable.
expected.notes: Tool exceptions are caught by the dispatcher and returned as error strings. The loop continues  this is a success path. Events verify type sequence only (payloads vary by runtime).

name: cancellation_before_llm
description: Â§13.2 Cancellation â Cancel token is already cancelled before the first LLM call. No LLM calls are made and CancelledError is raised.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

expected.notes: No LLM calls are made. The cancel token is checked at the top of each iteration. Since it is already cancelled, the loop exits immediately with CancelledError.

name: cancellation_between_tools
description: Â§13.2 Cancellation â LLM requests 2 tool calls. Cancel fires after the first tool executes. The second tool must NOT be called.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Compare weather in Paris and London.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

expected.notes: Cancel is checked between tool executions. The first tool (Paris) completes, then the cancel token fires. The second tool (London) must NOT be executed. Only one tool_result event is emitted.

name: cancellation_between_iterations
description: Â§13.2 Cancellation â Turn 1 completes (tool call + result). Cancel fires before turn 2. The loop exits with CancelledError after 1 iteration.

messages.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

expected.notes: Iteration 1 runs to completion (LLM call + tool execution + message append). The cancel token is checked at the top of iteration 2, causing CancelledError. The 5 messages are: system, user, assistant(tool_calls), tool(result), but the second LLM call never happens.

name: context_trim_basic
description: Â§13.3 Context Window â Messages exceed the context budget. Oldest user/assistant pairs are dropped and a summary is inserted after the system message.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Tell me about the history of Rome. I want a very detailed answer covering all major periods.

Rome was founded in 753 BC. The Roman Kingdom lasted until 509 BC when the Republic was established. The Republic expanded through the Punic Wars and conquered the Mediterranean. Julius Caesar rose to power and was assassinated in 44 BC. Augustus became the first Emperor in 27 BC, beginning the Roman Empire.
Now tell me about the fall of Rome. Again, very detailed with all the key events and dates.

The decline of Rome began in the 3rd century with the Crisis of the Third Century. Diocletian split the empire in 285 AD. Constantine founded Constantinople in 330 AD. The Western Empire fell in 476 AD when Odoacer deposed Romulus Augustulus. The Eastern Empire continued as the Byzantine Empire until 1453.
Tell me about medieval Europe after Rome fell. Cover the major kingdoms and events.

After Rome fell, Europe entered the Early Middle Ages. The Franks under Clovis established a kingdom in Gaul. Charlemagne united much of Western Europe and was crowned Emperor in 800 AD. The Viking Age began in 793 AD. The feudal system developed across Europe. The High Middle Ages saw the Crusades beginning in 1095.
Now tell me about the Renaissance period.

The Renaissance began in Italy in the 14th century. Florence was the center under the Medici family. Key figures included Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Raphael. The printing press was invented by Gutenberg around 1440. The Renaissance spread north to France, England, and the Low Countries. It marked a transition from medieval to modern thinking.
Finally, what is the weather in Paris today?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

expected.result: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

trimmed_messages.content: [Summary of earlier conversation] User asked about the history of Rome, the fall of Rome, medieval Europe, and the Renaissance period.
Finally, what is the weather in Paris today?

expected.notes: Context trimming drops oldest user/assistant pairs to fit within the 500-character budget. The system message is always preserved. A summary message (role=system) is injected after the original system message. The most recent user message is always kept.

name: context_no_trim_when_fits
description: Â§13.3 Context Window â Messages fit within the context budget. No trimming occurs and all messages are preserved.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is 2 + 2?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: 2 + 2 equals 4.

expected.result: 2 + 2 equals 4.
expected.notes: When messages fit within the context_budget (10000 characters), no trimming occurs. The trimmed_messages field is null to indicate no modification was needed.

name: context_preserves_system_messages
description: Â§13.3 Context Window â Two system messages plus many user/assistant pairs. After trimming, both system messages MUST still be present.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Always respond in a formal tone.
Tell me about ancient Egypt and all the pharaohs and their dynasties in great detail.

Ancient Egypt's history spans over 3000 years. The Old Kingdom saw the building of the Great Pyramids at Giza under pharaohs Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure. The Middle Kingdom was a period of reunification. The New Kingdom included famous rulers like Hatshepsut, Thutmose III, Akhenaten, and Tutankhamun.
Now tell me about Greek philosophy covering all major philosophers and their contributions.

Greek philosophy began with the Pre-Socratics. Socrates developed the Socratic method. Plato founded the Academy and wrote The Republic. Aristotle studied under Plato and founded the Lyceum, contributing to logic, metaphysics, ethics, and natural sciences. The Stoics and Epicureans followed in the Hellenistic period.
What is the weather today?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: I would need to know your city to provide the weather.

expected.result: I would need to know your city to provide the weather.

trimmed_messages.content: Always respond in a formal tone.
[Summary of earlier conversation] User asked about ancient Egypt and pharaohs, then about Greek philosophy and major philosophers.

expected.notes: ALL system messages are preserved during context trimming â they are never dropped regardless of budget. A summary is inserted after the last system message. The most recent user message is always kept.

name: guardrail_input_deny
description: Â§13.4 Guardrails â Input guardrail denies the request before any LLM call is made. Returns GuardrailError with the denial reason.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
My SSN is 123-45-6789, can you look that up?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

expected.notes: Input guardrail runs before the first LLM call. When it returns action=deny, the loop immediately raises GuardrailError with the provided reason. No LLM calls or tool executions occur.

name: guardrail_output_deny
description: Â§13.4 Guardrails â Input guardrail passes, LLM returns a response, but the output guardrail denies it. Returns GuardrailError.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Tell me something.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

message.content: Here is some problematic content that the guardrail catches.

expected.notes: The input guardrail passes (action=allow). The LLM returns a response. The output guardrail runs on the response and returns action=deny, causing GuardrailError. The LLM was called once but the result is not returned to the user.

name: guardrail_tool_deny
description: Â§13.4 Guardrails â LLM requests 2 tool calls. Tool guardrail denies one (dangerous_tool) but allows the other (get_weather). Denied tool gets a synthetic error result.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Get the weather and also run the dangerous operation.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tools.name: dangerous_tool
tools.description: A dangerous operation that should be guarded

tool_results.result: Tool denied by guardrail: Tool not authorized

message.content: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny. I was unable to run the dangerous operation as it was not authorized.

expected.result: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny. I was unable to run the dangerous operation as it was not authorized.
expected.notes: The tool guardrail intercepts tool calls before execution. get_weather is allowed and executes normally. dangerous_tool is denied â its tool result message contains a synthetic denial string instead of actual execution output. Both results are sent to the LLM so it can respond appropriately.

name: guardrail_all_pass
description: Â§13.4 Guardrails â All guardrails (input, output, tool) are configured but all pass. The agent loop completes normally.

messages.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

message.content: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.

expected.result: The weather in Paris is 72Â°F and sunny.
expected.notes: All three guardrail types are configured but none deny. Input guardrail allows, tool guardrail has an empty deny list, and output guardrail allows. The agent loop completes as if no guardrails were present.

name: steering_inject_message
description: Â§13.5 Steering â After turn 1 (tool call + result), a steering message is injected before turn 2's LLM call. The LLM sees the injected message in context.

messages.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

messages.text: Actually, focus on the temperature in Celsius.

message.content: The temperature in Paris is approximately 22Â°C and it is sunny.

expected.result: The temperature in Paris is approximately 22Â°C and it is sunny.

data.response: The temperature in Paris is approximately 22Â°C and it is sunny.

message_sequence.content: You are a helpful weather assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?
72Â°F sunny
Actually, focus on the temperature in Celsius.
The temperature in Paris is approximately 22Â°C and it is sunny.

expected.notes: The steering message is injected as a user message before iteration 2's LLM call. total_messages is 7 (system + user + assistant_tool + tool_result + steering_user + assistant_final = 6 shown, but 7 when including the final assistant). The steering message appears between the tool result and the final LLM call.

name: steering_multiple_messages
description: Â§13.5 Steering â Two steering messages are queued and both injected before iteration 2. Tests atomic drain â both appear in order.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tool_functions.get_weather: returns weather string for the requested city

message.content: Le temps Ã  Paris est de 22Â°C et ensoleillÃ© avec une humiditÃ© de 45%.

expected.result: Le temps Ã  Paris est de 22Â°C et ensoleillÃ© avec une humiditÃ© de 45%.

message_sequence.content: You are a helpful assistant.
What is the weather in Paris?
72Â°F sunny, 45% humidity
Please respond in French.
Also include the humidity percentage.
Le temps Ã  Paris est de 22Â°C et ensoleillÃ© avec une humiditÃ© de 45%.

expected.notes: Both steering messages are drained atomically and inserted in order before iteration 2's LLM call. They appear as consecutive user messages between the tool result and the final assistant response. total_messages is 8 (system + user + assistant_tool + tool_result + steer1 + steer2 + assistant_final = 7 content messages, but counting from 1-indexed gives 8 including all roles).

name: parallel_tools_basic
description: Â§13.6 Parallel Tools â LLM requests 3 tool calls in one turn. All execute (potentially in parallel) and results are returned in the same order as the requests.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant with access to weather, time, and news tools.
Give me the weather in Paris, the current time in Tokyo, and the latest news.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tools.name: get_time
tools.description: Get the current time in a city

tools.name: get_news
tools.description: Get the latest news headlines

tool_results.result: Tech stocks rise 5%. New climate accord signed. Mars rover discovers ice.

message.content: Here's your update: Paris is 72Â°F and sunny. The time in Tokyo is 3:45 PM JST. Latest news: Tech stocks rise 5%, a new climate accord was signed, and the Mars rover discovered ice.

expected.rust_expected_error: parallel_tool_calls=true is not supported by the canonical Rust engine
expected.result: Here's your update: Paris is 72Â°F and sunny. The time in Tokyo is 3:45 PM JST. Latest news: Tech stocks rise 5%, a new climate accord was signed, and the Mars rover discovered ice.
expected.notes: With parallel_tool_calls=true, all 3 tool calls may execute concurrently. Regardless of execution order, tool result messages must be appended in the same order as the original tool_calls array. The total is 8 messages: system + user + assistant(3 tool_calls) + 3 tool results + final assistant.

name: parallel_tools_with_guardrail_deny
description: Â§13.6 Parallel Tools + Â§13.4 Guardrails â 3 parallel tool calls, one denied by tool guardrail. The 2 allowed tools execute, the denied tool gets a synthetic result.

messages.content: You are a helpful assistant.
Get weather, time, and run the dangerous operation.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get the current weather for a city

tools.name: get_time
tools.description: Get the current time in a city

tools.name: dangerous_tool
tools.description: A dangerous operation

tool_results.result: Tool denied by guardrail: Not authorized

message.content: Paris is 72Â°F and sunny. The time in Tokyo is 3:45 PM JST. The dangerous operation could not be executed as it is not authorized.

expected.rust_expected_error: parallel_tool_calls=true is not supported by the canonical Rust engine
expected.result: Paris is 72Â°F and sunny. The time in Tokyo is 3:45 PM JST. The dangerous operation could not be executed as it is not authorized.
expected.notes: The tool guardrail filters dangerous_tool before execution. get_weather and get_time execute (potentially in parallel). dangerous_tool receives a synthetic denial result. All 3 tool result messages are appended in the original tool_calls order. The denied tool is never actually called but its result slot is filled with the denial message.
